title: cratoxylum biflorum
intro: Cratoxylum biflorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Hypericaceae, commonly known as the two-flowered cratoxylum or two-flowered st. john's wort. The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine, particularly in the Philippines and Indonesia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cratoxylum biflorum include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory tract infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea infusions, tinctures, and capsules, which can be made from the leaves and flowers of the plant.
